mechanism_narrative: A trailing 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. This signal is always active: the magnitude of the change is the predictor.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in FEDFUNDS has a Spearman rank correlation with subsequent 20-day forward returns less than 0.05 over any rolling 3-year window, or the sign of the correlation is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that puts upward pressure on wages and inflation, leading to monetary tightening and negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 5.0% in a given month
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE < 5.0%, the average 5-day forward return is not negative or is not significantly different from periods when UNRATE >= 5.0% (two-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong real retail sales growth (RSAFS YoY > 5%) and declining federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS 3-month change negative) jointly indicate robust aggregate demand and accommodative policy, producing positive equity returns over 20 trading days. The signal requires both conditions.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 5% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change < 0
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, the subsequent 20-day forward returns are not positive (median return < 0) and the proportion of positive instances is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (rising FEDFUNDS), acceleration in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals overheating and raises the probability of further policy tightening, leading to negative equity returns over 60 trading days. The direction of RSAFS signal is conditional on the monetary regime.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased by at least 25bps over the past 3 months
failure_condition: In tightening cycles, the correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent 60-day forward returns is not negative, or the average return when RSAFS growth is above its median is not lower than when below (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding aggregate supply and economic health, which consistently predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is an always-on trend: higher month-over-month growth indicates higher forward returns.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year period with the top 20% of INDPRO monthly changes shows median forward 20-day return equal to or lower than the bottom 20% period, or the Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO change and forward return is less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) gain below 100,000 signals labor market weakness and a slowing economy, which often precedes equity drawdowns over the next quarter (60 trading days). This threshold triggers a negative market outlook.
failure_condition: In months following a PAYEMS gain below 100K, the average 60-day forward return is not negative, or the probability of a negative return is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts (HOUST > 1800K) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 2.5%) indicates a buoyant housing sector driving consumer wealth and demand, which jointly predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The interaction amplifies the signal.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the median 5-day forward return is zero or negative, or the positive hit rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPIAUCSL YoY < 3%), a month-over-month acceleration in consumer prices signals rising demand without immediate tightening risk, leading to positive equity returns over 20 trading days. The direction reverses in high inflation regimes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase from previous month
failure_condition: During periods with CPI YoY < 3%, the correlation between monthly CPI acceleration and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or indistinguishable from zero (|rho| < 0.05).
